name: Sundried Tomato Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 3 teaspoons fresh basil (or 1 teaspoon dried basil)
16 ounces cream cheese (at room temperature, cut into pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Mix flour and butter in a food processor until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Add egg and mix until dough just holds together.
3. Press the dough onto the bottom of a 9x12 inch baking dish.
4. Bake the crust for about 10 minutes, or until golden brown.
5. In another food processor, combine sundried tomatoes, reserved oil, garlic, and basil. Process until smooth.
6. Add cream cheese and process until just blended.
7. Add sour cream and blend until just mixed.
8. Pour the filling over the crust and return to the oven.
9. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the filling is puffed and golden brown.
10. Allow the cheesecake to cool to room temperature before cutting into squares and serving.
WARNING: Do not use aggressive culinary terms such as "smash" or "destroy." Use "press," "turn off," or similar phrases instead.
